# MediaLocker

> MediaLocker is cloud object storage built for media creators — buckets, media tagging, sets (variant renditions), storyboards (clip sequences), per-organization usage & billing, and short-lived presigned URLs. Object bytes always move directly between the client and storage over signed URLs; the platform is never a byte proxy. Three programmable surfaces share one credential and scope model: a REST API, a hosted MCP server for AI agents, and the `medialocker` CLI.

Authentication is identical across all three surfaces: an API key sent as `Authorization: Bearer <secret-access-key>`. Keys carry scopes (`read`, `write`, `delete`, `admin`) plus an optional per-credential tool/bucket allowlist. Create keys in the dashboard under "API Keys" or via `POST /api/api-keys`. A single expanded document with the full text of these instructions is at https://docs.medialocker.io/llms-full.txt.

## REST API

- [API overview](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/): base URL `https://api.medialocker.io`, endpoints under `/api`
- [API reference](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/api-reference): all endpoints — buckets, media, search, tags, categories, sets, storyboards, usage, billing
- [OpenAPI 3.0 spec](https://api.medialocker.io/api/openapi.json): machine-readable schema
- [Authentication & scopes](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/authentication): bearer API keys and the read/write/delete/admin model
- [Presigned uploads](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/presign): single-shot and multipart direct-to-storage flow
- [Idempotency](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/idempotency): the `Idempotency-Key` header for safe retries
- [Rate limits](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/rate-limits): 300 requests/minute per organization
- [Errors](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/errors): error envelope and HTTP status codes

## MCP Server (for AI agents)

- [MCP overview](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/): endpoint `https://mcp.medialocker.io/mcp`, Streamable HTTP / JSON-RPC with `Mcp-Session-Id`
- [Connecting](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/connecting): client config for Claude, Cursor, and other MCP clients
- [Authentication](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/authentication): the same API keys, scope-gated tools, and per-credential allowlist
- [Tools](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/tools): search / bucket / object / tag / set / usage tools, plus firewall-gated destructive tools
- [Resources](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/resources): read-only `medialocker://` context URIs
- [Rate limits](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/mcp/rate-limits): 120 requests/minute, 50,000/day per tenant
- [Server llms.txt](https://mcp.medialocker.io/llms.txt): the MCP server's own live, version-pinned discovery document

## CLI

- [CLI overview](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/): install with `npm install -g medialocker`, binary `medialocker`, Node.js ≥ 22
- [Installation](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/installation): npm / pnpm / yarn / `npx medialocker`
- [Configuration & auth](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/configuration): `medialocker login`, env vars, and named profiles
- [Command reference](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/commands): buckets, media, search, tags, categories, sets, storyboards, usage, billing, apikeys
- [Uploading & downloading](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/uploads): automatic single-shot / multipart presigned transfers
- [Local MCP server](https://docs.medialocker.io/developer/cli/mcp): `medialocker mcp serve` (stdio) and `medialocker mcp install`

## User Guide

- [User guide](https://docs.medialocker.io/user/): dashboard workflow — buckets, uploads, organizing, usage & billing
- [API keys](https://docs.medialocker.io/user/api-keys): creating and scoping keys

## Optional

- [Self-hosting](https://docs.medialocker.io/self-hosting/): Docker Compose, storage, wildcard TLS, scaling, backups
- [App source (AGPL-3.0)](https://github.com/medialocker/medialocker-app)
- [CLI source (AGPL-3.0)](https://github.com/medialocker/medialocker-cli)
